rejected.reason: libheif (github.com/strukturag/libheif) is a C/C++ library, not a Go module; transcribing it as ecosystem=Go means it will never match in the Go module proxy and would mislead a Go agent.
fixed: null while the fix text instructs 'upgrade past the fixed version' is an internal contradiction — no fixed version exists for kubeview so the upgrade instruction is misleading.
source_url and fix URL both reference GHSA-gvm4-2qqg-m333 (moby/libnetwork), not the documented advisory GHSA-232p-vwff-86mp; the provenance URL does not match the advisory being recorded.

The github.com/filebrowser/filebrowser (non-v2) entry has fixed: null while the single fix instruction says 'upgrade past the fixed version', creating the same null-fixed contradiction as exp-0012 and potentially misleading agents about the v1 code line.

Meaning/poison failure: the fix URL and source_url both reference a different GHSA ID (GHSA-vv24-rm95-q56r in jaeger-ui), not the recorded GHSA-2w8w-qhg4-f78j in jaegertracing/jaeger — a future agent following the fix link would read a mismatched advisory
Meaning/poison failure: package field contains 'https://github.com/dadrus/heimdall' — the https:// prefix makes this an invalid Go module path that will never match any real Go module lookup

Two of the four applies_to entries use github.com/prometheus/prometheus/v2, a fabricated major-version suffix — Prometheus does not publish a /v2 Go module, so these entries would never match the real package and would mislead a Go agent.
fixed: null contradicts the fix text which instructs upgrading past the fixed version — there is no fixed version to upgrade past, making the resolution instruction actively misleading to a Go agent.

Meaning failure: the record title and error_signatures carry GHSA-77vh-xpmg-72qh against package github.com/opencontainers/image-spec, but both the source_url and the fix URL point to a completely different advisory (GHSA-mc8v-mgrf-8f4m) on a different repo (distribution-spec); the recorded GHSA id and the cited source are mismatched, making this internally inconsistent and misleading.

Internal contradiction: both package entries have fixed=null yet the fix text says 'upgrade past the fixed version'; additionally the /v2 suffix on the second package entry may be fabricated if the project does not publish a v2 module — fails meaning and poison checks.

Package path github.com/traefik/traefik omits the required major-version suffixes (/v2 for the 2.x range, /v3 for the 3.x range) that Traefik actually uses; a Go agent would never match these references — fails meaning and poison checks.

Internal contradiction: fixed=null yet the fix text instructs 'upgrade past the fixed version', which is false and misleading when no fix version exists — fails meaning and poison checks.
